name: Lighthouse

ocean: Pacific Ocean, Bali Sea, Lombok Strait
description: This dive site features a stunning coral-covered slope with vibrant soft and hard corals. Strong currents attract pelagic species like manta rays, sharks, and large schools of fish. The underwater topography includes dramatic drop-offs and swim-throughs. Visibility is often excellent, making it ideal for drift diving. The site is known for its rich biodiversity and thrilling encounters with marine life. Suitable for experienced divers due to the currents.

name: Mirkos
region: Asia, Indonesia, West Nusa Tenggara, Lombok, North Lombok Regency, Pemenang, Gili Islands, Gili Air
description: A vibrant reef slopes gently to 18 meters, teeming with colorful coral and schools of tropical fish. Turtles frequently glide past, and occasional reef sharks patrol deeper sections. The sandy bottom near the shore transitions to healthy hard corals, perfect for macro photography. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 20 meters. Currents are mild, making it ideal for beginners. The site sits just off Gili Air, offering easy access from shore or boat.

description: Shark Point off Gili Trawangan offers thrilling drift dives along a vibrant coral wall teeming with reef sharks, turtles, and schools of barracuda. Strong currents attract pelagics, making it ideal for advanced divers. The steep drop-off reveals stunning soft corals and occasional manta rays. Visibility often exceeds 30 meters, with depths ranging from 10 to 40 meters. A must-dive for adrenaline seekers and marine life enthusiasts.

description: This site off Gili Meno features a sloping reef with vibrant coral gardens and frequent sightings of reef sharks, turtles, and schools of tropical fish. The current is mild, making it ideal for divers of all levels. The sandy bottom transitions to a dramatic drop-off, where larger pelagics like manta rays occasionally pass by. Visibility is excellent, often exceeding 20 meters. The shallow sections are perfect for snorkelers, while deeper areas offer exciting exploration. Night dives reveal octopuses and crustaceans.

description: Strong currents sweep divers along a vibrant reef wall teeming with life. Expect schooling jacks, reef sharks, and eagle rays patrolling the blue. Hard and soft corals thrive in the nutrient-rich flow. Depth drops to 30m, best for advanced divers. Visibility often exceeds 20m. Drift diving only—no moorings. Accessible by boat from Gili Trawangan.

The shallow reefs are packed with life, while deeper sections reveal occasional reef sharks. A hidden gem for critter lovers.

name: Trawangan Slope
region: Asia, Indonesia, West Nusa Tenggara, Lombok, North Lombok Regency, Pemenang
description: This vibrant slope dive features a gentle descent along coral-covered terrain teeming with marine life. Expect schools of batfish, reef sharks, and turtles gliding past colorful soft corals. The current is mild, making it ideal for all skill levels. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, revealing a thriving ecosystem. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. The sandy bottom transitions to healthy reefs, with occasional sightings of eagle rays. Easy access from shore or boat adds to its appeal.

name: Setangi Rock
region: Asia, Indonesia, West Nusa Tenggara, Lombok
ocean: Pacific Ocean, Bali Sea, Lombok Strait
description: Setangi Rock offers a thrilling drift dive along a submerged pinnacle in the Lombok Strait. Strong currents attract large pelagics like manta rays, reef sharks, and schooling trevallies. The site features dramatic underwater topography with steep walls, vibrant soft corals, and occasional thermoclines. Depth ranges from 10 to 30 meters, best suited for experienced divers. Visibility often exceeds 20 meters, with nutrient-rich waters supporting diverse marine life. Night dives reveal octopus and crustaceans. The site sits near the famous Gili Islands, making it accessible for liveaboards or day trips from Lombok.
